chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with s0md3v/XSStrike (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with s0md3v/ReconDog (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the frameworks category. Fits when the engagement needs: Incredibly fast crawler designed for OSINT.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Incredibly fast crawler designed for OSINT.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/s0md3v/Photon.git; docker run -it --name photon photon:latest -u google.com. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `docker build -t photon .`. Further invocations from upstream: `docker run -it --name photon photon:latest -u google.com`; `docker run -it --name photon -v "$PWD:/Photon/google.com" photon:latest -u google.com`. Incredibly fast crawler designed for OSINT.

name: Photon
summary: Incredibly fast crawler designed for OSINT.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need frameworks, use Photon because Incredibly fast crawler designed for OSINT.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `docker build -t photon .` as documented upstream for Photon.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `docker run -it --name photon photon:latest -u google.com` as documented upstream for Photon.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `docker run -it --name photon -v "$PWD:/Photon/google.com" photon:latest -u google.com` as documented upstream for Photon.
When chaining frameworks tools, pair Photon with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Photon --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
